claim: Progressive discovery pattern defers loading of tools and loads them on-demand using tool search, dramatically reducing initial context window usage
actionable: Implement tool search or similar mechanism to load only tool descriptions initially, fetching full details only when the model needs them
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic

claim: Programmatic tool calling (code execution mode) is more efficient than sequential tool invocations because it lets the model compose operations in one call instead of bouncing between tools
actionable: Give models a REPL or execution environment instead of individual tools, allowing them to write and execute code that chains operations together
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) has reached 110 million monthly downloads, becoming a common standard across OpenAI, Google, LangChain and thousands of frameworks
actionable: Treat MCP as a standard interop layer when building agent infrastructure; assume diverse clients and frameworks will consume MCP servers
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic

claim: 2025 was about exploring agents; 2026 is about production agents that require connectivity through skills, MCP, and CLI/computer use together, not one tool alone
actionable: Design production agents with a hybrid connectivity stack using all three (skills, MCP, CLI) based on the task rather than picking one
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic

claim: Design MCP servers for agents first (as you would for humans), not as 1:1 REST-to-MCP conversions, and use rich semantics like MCP applications and tasks
actionable: Stop wrapping REST APIs directly in MCP; instead design server interfaces for agentic use with rich semantics, execution environments, and task support
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic

claim: Skills over MCP will allow servers to ship updated domain knowledge without relying on plugin mechanisms or registries, enabling continuous skill updates
actionable: Use skills over MCP to encode domain knowledge and instructions alongside tools, allowing versioned knowledge updates without registry changes
source_title: The Future of MCP — David Soria Parra, Anthropic
